key_themes: solar and wind power capacity increase
notable_excerpts: We expect over the next 2 years, the wind and solar generation capex to be in the order of around Rs. 33,000 – Rs. 34,000 crore.
The entire ecosystem will support a 10 gigawatt capacity.

For electrolyzers, we have started the work and we expect to give you an update as to how that work is proceeding in terms of construction and development of the electrolyzer facility by March next year.
In the airport business, the growth in passenger movements has resulted in Adani Airport Holding handling roughly 23% of India's passenger movements.
During the quarter, we also tested the southern runway of Navi Mumbai airport with the landing of Air Force plane and we expect the airport to be completed early 2025.
For full year capex, I will go through various segments. So in New Industries, we expect the full year capex to be around Rs. 28,000 crore.
We expect the roads to complete its projects on schedule with capex of roughly around Rs. 12,000 crore.

chain_of_thought: The earnings call highlighted significant environmental initiatives, particularly in renewable energy and green hydrogen. Social aspects were addressed through infrastructure projects like airports, which enhance community connectivity and employment. Governance was discussed in terms of capex management and project timelines, but lacked depth in areas like board independence and ethics.
strengths: Strong focus on renewable energy and green hydrogen.
Significant infrastructure projects enhancing community connectivity.
weaknesses: Limited discussion on governance aspects like board independence and ethics.
